chunks.text:
# Manufacturing Business Glossary

This glossary defines the core business concepts and terminology used in the manufacturing supply chain domain. It covers product structures, materials management, production workflows, and quality assurance processes.

## Product

A Product represents any manufactured item in the system, including finished goods ready for sale, sub-assemblies used in production, and individual components. Products are organized in a hierarchical structure where complex product... [TRUNCATED]

## Warehouse

A Warehouse represents a physical storage location where materials, components, and finished goods are stored. Warehouses have defined capacity limits, geographic location information, and are managed by designated managers. Warehouses serve as nodes in the supply chain network where inventory is held, shipments are received, and products are staged for production or distribution.

**Key Attributes:**
- Unique warehouse identifier
- Warehouse name and location
- Address, city, and ... [TRUNCATED]

## QualityControl

Quality Control (QC) represents inspection and testing activities performed on materials, in-process production, or finished goods. QC records track the type of inspection (incoming, in-process, final), inspection date, inspector, test results (pass, fail, conditional), defect counts, and notes. QC activities are associated with specific production batches and may reference technical specifications being tested.

**Key Attributes:**
- Unique QC identifier
- Batch reference (pr... [TRUNCATED]

# Manufacturing Data Dictionary

This data dictionary documents the database schema for the manufacturing supply chain system. It includes all tables, columns, data types, constraints, and relationships. Special attention is given to self-referential relationships that support hierarchical product structures.

## product

Stores all manufactured items including finished goods, assemblies, and components. Supports hierarchical product relationships via self-referencing foreign key.

| Column | Da... [TRUNCATED]

## bom

Bill of Materials defining product composition and hierarchical structure. Supports recursive relationships for multi-level assemblies.

| Column | Data Type | Constraints | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| bom_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique BOM identifier |
| parent_product_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Parent product (what is being built) |
| component_product_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → produc... [TRUNCATED]

## warehouse

Stores physical storage location information for inventory management.

| Column | Data Type | Constraints | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| warehouse_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique warehouse identifier |
| warehouse_name | VARCHAR(100) | NOT NULL | Warehouse name |
| address | VARCHAR(200) | | Street address |
| city | VARCHAR(50) | | City location |
| state | VARCHAR(50) | | State or province |
| capacity | INT | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0, CH... [TRUNCATED]

## work_order

Represents production jobs for manufacturing products with hierarchical work order support.

| Column | Data Type | Constraints | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| work_order_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique work order identifier |
| product_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Product to manufacture |
| parent_work_order | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → work_order(work_order_id) | Parent work order for decomposition... [TRUNCATED]

## specification

Defines technical requirements and acceptance criteria for materials and products.

| Column | Data Type | Constraints | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| specification_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique specification identifier |
| specification_name | VARCHAR(100) | NOT NULL | Specification name |
| version | VARCHAR(10) | NOT NULL | Specification version |
| effective_date | DATE | NOT NULL | Effective date |
| spec_type | spec_type_t | ... [TRUNCATED]

## shipment

Tracks material transfers between warehouses, suppliers, and customers.

| Column | Data Type | Constraints | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| shipment_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique shipment identifier |
| shipment_type | shipment_type_t | NOT NULL | INBOUND, OUTBOUND, INTERNAL |
| warehouse_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → warehouse(warehouse_id) | Warehouse reference |
| supplier_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → supplier(supplier_id) | S... [TRUNCATED]

entities_post_resolution.name: Batch
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Batch is a specific production lot or group of units produced together in a single manufacturing cycle, used for tracking and quality control purposes.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: A Batch represents a specific production lot or run of a product, tracking all units produced together in a single manufacturing cycle. | - QC inspections are performed on Batches | Batches record the product produced, production date, quantity produced, warehouse location, expiry date (if applicable), and quality control status. | - Batches produce a specific Product | - Batches are stored in Warehouses | - Batches undergo Quality Control inspections | - Batches may have expiry dates for perishable goods | - Batches enable traceability and recall management

entities_post_resolution.name: Component
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Component is an atomic raw material or purchased part used in manufacturing that cannot be further broken down into simpler items.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: A Component represents atomic raw materials or purchased parts that cannot be further broken down into simpler manufactured items. | Each component has a standard cost, unit of measure, and may be associated with technical specifications that define quality requirements. | Components are supplied by multiple Suppliers (many-to-many). | Components may reference Specifications for quality requirements. | component_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → component(component_id) | Component reference | component_product_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Component product (what goes into it) | Stores atomic raw materials and purchased parts that are procured from external suppliers.

entities_post_resolution.name: Contact Email Address
entities_post_resolution.definition: A contact email address is an electronic mailing address used to communicate with an individual or organization.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: contact_email | VARCHAR(100) | | Contact email address

entities_post_resolution.name: Inventory
entities_post_resolution.definition: An inventory is a collection of records that track stock levels, locations, and quantities of components and products stored in warehouses.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Inventory represents the actual stock levels of components and products stored in specific warehouses. | Each inventory record tracks the quantity on hand, quantity reserved for pending orders, and reorder thresholds to trigger procurement. | Inventory records support either components or products (but not both in the same record) and include timestamps for the last restock date. | Products are stored in Inventory records. | Tracks stock levels for components and products at specific warehouse locations. | | inventory_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique inventory identifier |

entities_post_resolution.name: Parent Product
entities_post_resolution.definition: A parent product is a product that serves as an assembly or component in a hierarchical structure, referencing other products as its parts.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: parent_product_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Parent product (what is being built) | A Product can reference a parent Product (for multi-level assemblies). | Each BOM entry indicates a parent product, a component product (which can be either a component or sub-assembly), the required quantity, unit of measure, and hierarchical level in the product structure. | parent_product_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Parent product for hierarchical assemblies | product_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique product identifier | | product_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Product reference (exclusive with component_id) | | | product_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Product to manufacture |

entities_post_resolution.name: Planned Start Date
entities_post_resolution.definition: These entities represent the scheduled dates marking the start and end of a planned activity or project.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | planned_end_date | DATE | | Planned end date | | | planned_start_date | DATE | | Planned start date |

entities_post_resolution.name: Product
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Product is any manufactured item in the system, including finished goods, assemblies, and components, with attributes such as name, type, cost, and lead time.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: A Product represents any manufactured item in the system, including finished goods ready for sale, sub-assemblies used in production, and individual components. | Each product has a unique identifier, name, type classification, base cost, and lead time for procurement or production. | A Product can have multiple child Products (via BOM hierarchy). | A Product can reference a parent Product (for multi-level assemblies). | Products are organized in a hierarchical structure where complex products can contain simpler products as parts. | Products are used in Work Orders for production. | Products appear in Shipments (outbound). | Products are stored in Inventory records. | BOM connects Products to other Products (parent-child). | Stores all manufactured items including finished goods, assemblies, and components. | Supports hierarchical product relationships via self-referencing foreign key. | product_name | VARCHAR(100) | NOT NULL | Product name | product_type | product_type_t | NOT NULL,

entities_post_resolution.name: Quantity Completed
entities_post_resolution.definition: Quantity completed is a business metric indicating the total amount of work or items finished within a process or transaction.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | quantity_completed | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0, <= ordered | Quantity completed |

entities_post_resolution.name: Shipment
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Shipment is the physical transfer of materials between locations such as warehouses, suppliers, and customers within a supply chain.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: A Shipment represents the physical transfer of materials between warehouses, from suppliers to warehouses (inbound), or from warehouses to customers (outbound). | Products appear in Shipments (outbound). | Shipments connect Warehouses, Suppliers, and Customers.

entities_post_resolution.name: Specification
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Specification is a document that defines technical requirements, standards, and acceptance criteria for materials or products.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: A Specification defines technical requirements, standards, and acceptance criteria for materials or products. | Components may reference Specifications for quality requirements. | - QC tests against Specifications | Specifications include version numbers, effective dates, specification types (dimensional, material, performance), critical parameters to test, and acceptable value ranges with units of measure. | Specifications ensure manufactured products meet engineering and customer requirements.

entities_post_resolution.name: Storage Capacity
entities_post_resolution.definition: Storage capacity refers to the maximum amount of data that can be stored in a storage device or system.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | capacity | INT | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0, CHECK > 0 | Storage capacity |

entities_post_resolution.name: Street Address
entities_post_resolution.definition: A street address is a specific location identifier used to pinpoint a physical location for mailing or navigation purposes.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | address | VARCHAR(200) | | Street address |

entities_post_resolution.name: Supplier
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Supplier is an external vendor or company that provides raw materials, components, or finished goods to the manufacturing organization.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: A Supplier is an external vendor or company that provides raw materials, components, or finished goods to the manufacturing organization. | supplier_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → supplier(supplier_id) | Supplier reference | supplier_name | VARCHAR(100) | NOT NULL | Supplier name | supplier_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique supplier identifier | Suppliers are linked to Inbound Shipments. | Suppliers have negotiated pricing and lead times per component.

entities_post_resolution.name: Supplier
entities_post_resolution.definition: A supplier is an entity that provides goods or services to a business, identified by a unique supplier ID and associated with a supplier name.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: A Supplier is an external vendor or company that provides raw materials, components, or finished goods to the manufacturing organization. | supplier_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → supplier(supplier_id) | Supplier reference | supplier_name | VARCHAR(100) | NOT NULL | Supplier name | supplier_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique supplier identifier

entities_post_resolution.name: Unique Inventory Identifier
entities_post_resolution.definition: A unique identifier assigned to a specific inventory item, used to distinguish it within inventory management systems.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | inventory_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique inventory identifier | | supplier_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique supplier identifier | | warehouse_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique warehouse identifier | | Each product has a unique identifier, name, type classification, base cost, and lead time for procurement or production.

entities_post_resolution.name: Unit Of Measure
entities_post_resolution.definition: A unit of measure is a standard quantity used to specify the amount or size of a product or item in business transactions.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: unit_of_measure | VARCHAR(10) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'EA' | Unit of measure | unit_of_measure | VARCHAR(10) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'EA' | Unit of measure (EA, KG, M, L, etc.)

entities_post_resolution.name: Warehouse
entities_post_resolution.definition: A warehouse is a physical storage location where materials, components, and finished goods are stored, managed, and tracked within a supply chain.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: A Warehouse represents a physical storage location where materials, components, and finished goods are stored. | | warehouse_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → warehouse(warehouse_id) | Warehouse location | | | warehouse_name | VARCHAR(100) | NOT NULL | Warehouse name | | Warehouses have defined capacity limits, geographic location information, and are managed by designated managers. | Warehouses serve as nodes in the supply chain network where inventory is held, shipments are received, and products are staged for production or distribution. | - Batches are stored in Warehouses | Stores physical storage location information for inventory management. | | warehouse_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique warehouse identifier | | | warehouse_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → warehouse(warehouse_id) | Production location |

entities_post_resolution.name: Work Order
entities_post_resolution.definition: A work order is a formal document or record that authorizes and details a specific manufacturing or production task to be completed.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Products are used in Work Orders for production. | Work Orders track planned versus completed quantities, status through the production lifecycle, priority levels, and planned dates. | Work Orders can have hierarchical relationships where a parent work order may decompose into child work orders for different production stages or sub-assemblies. | A Work Order represents a production job or manufacturing run to produce a specified quantity of a product. | Represents production jobs for manufacturing products with hierarchical work order support. | | work_order_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique work order identifier |

entities_post_resolution.name: Production Job
entities_post_resolution.definition: A production job is a specific manufacturing task or process assigned to produce a certain quantity of a product within a manufacturing system.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: A Work Order represents a production job or manufacturing run to produce a specified quantity of a product. | Represents production jobs for manufacturing products with hierarchical work order support.

entities_post_resolution.name: Standard Cost
entities_post_resolution.definition: A standard cost is a predetermined estimated expense associated with a component or procurement process used for budgeting and cost control.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Each component has a standard cost, unit of measure, and may be associated with technical specifications that define quality requirements. | standard_cost | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Standard procurement cost

entities_post_resolution.name: Manufactured Item
entities_post_resolution.definition: A manufactured item is any product or component that has been produced through a manufacturing process, including finished goods, sub-assemblies, and individual parts.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: A Product represents any manufactured item in the system, including finished goods ready for sale, sub-assemblies used in production, and individual components. | Stores all manufactured items including finished goods, assemblies, and components.

entities_post_resolution.name: Atomic Raw Materials
entities_post_resolution.definition: A category of basic raw materials and purchased parts that are procured from external suppliers and cannot be further broken down into simpler manufactured items.
synonyms: atomic raw materials or purchased parts
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Stores atomic raw materials and purchased parts that are procured from external suppliers. | A Component represents atomic raw materials or purchased parts that cannot be further broken down into simpler manufactured items.

entities_post_resolution.name: Component Supplier Id
entities_post_resolution.definition: A component_id is a unique identifier for a component in a system or database.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: component_supplier_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique relationship identifier | component_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique component identifier | component_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → component(component_id) | Component reference | | component_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → component(component_id) | Component reference (exclusive with product_id) | | component_product_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Component product (what goes into it)

entities_post_resolution.name: Hierarchical Product Relationships
entities_post_resolution.definition: A hierarchical product relationship is a structured connection where products or work orders are organized in parent-child relationships to represent dependencies or decomposition.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Supports hierarchical product relationships via self-referencing foreign key. | Work Orders can have hierarchical relationships where a parent work order may decompose into child work orders for different production stages or sub-assemblies.

entities_post_resolution.name: Priority
entities_post_resolution.definition: An attribute indicating the level of urgency or importance, typically with values like LOW, MEDIUM, HIGH, and URGENT.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | priority | priority_t | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'MEDIUM' | LOW, MEDIUM, HIGH, URGENT |

entities_post_resolution.name: Qc Type
entities_post_resolution.definition: qc_type is a classification indicating the stage or category of quality control in a process, such as INCOMING, IN_PROCESS, or FINAL.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | qc_type | qc_type_t | NOT NULL | INCOMING, IN_PROCESS, FINAL |

entities_post_resolution.name: Quantity On Hand
entities_post_resolution.definition: The quantity on hand is the current available amount of inventory items in stock, used for inventory management and order fulfillment.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | quantity_on_hand | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Available quantity | | Each inventory record tracks the quantity on hand, quantity reserved for pending orders, and reorder thresholds to trigger procurement.

entities_post_resolution.name: Status
entities_post_resolution.definition: A status indicator representing the current state of an entity, such as pending, in progress, completed, or closed.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | status | status_t | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'PENDING' | PENDING, IN_PROGRESS, COMPLETED, CLOSED |

entities_post_resolution.name: Last Restock Timestamp
entities_post_resolution.definition: The last restock timestamp is the date and time when the inventory was most recently replenished.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | last_restock_date | DATE | | Last restock timestamp |

entities_post_resolution.name: Null Default
entities_post_resolution.definition: A set of database column constraints specifying nullability, default values, and checks for various data attributes.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: base_cost | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Base manufacturing cost | standard_cost | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Standard procurement cost | product_name | VARCHAR(100) | NOT NULL | Product name | component_name | VARCHAR(100) | NOT NULL | Component name | unit_of_measure | VARCHAR(10) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'EA' | Unit of measure (EA, KG, M, L, etc.) | product_type | product_type_t | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'FINISHED_GOOD' | Product classification: FINISHED_GOOD, ASSEMBLY, COMPONENT | is_active | yes_no_t | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'Y' | Active status flag | lead_time_days | INT | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0, CHECK >= 0 | Procurement or production lead time

entities_post_resolution.name: Number Of Defects Found
entities_post_resolution.definition: The total number of defects identified during a process or inspection.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | defect_count | INT | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0, CHECK >= 0 | Number of defects found |

entities_post_resolution.name: Performance Rating
entities_post_resolution.definition: Performance rating is a numerical score, typically between 1.0 and 5.0, used to evaluate an individual's or entity's performance.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: rating | DECIMAL(3,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 3.00, CHECK 1.0-5.0 | Performance rating

entities_post_resolution.name: Reorder Trigger Level
entities_post_resolution.definition: Reorder trigger level is the inventory threshold at which a reorder is automatically initiated to replenish stock.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | reorder_threshold | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00 | Reorder trigger level |

entities_post_resolution.name: Manufacturing Route
entities_post_resolution.definition: A manufacturing route is a defined sequence of operations and steps required to produce a product, used for planning and scheduling in manufacturing.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: A Route defines the sequence of manufacturing operations or steps required to produce a product. | Each route step specifies the operation name, work center where the operation is performed, cycle time for the operation, setup time required, and sequence number. | Routes collectively define the manufacturing workflow and are used for production scheduling, capacity planning, and cost calculation.

entities_post_resolution.name: Available Quantity
entities_post_resolution.definition: | quantity_on_hand | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Available quantity |
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | quantity_on_hand | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Available quantity |

entities_post_resolution.name: Bom
entities_post_resolution.definition: The Bill of Materials (BOM) defines the composition of products by specifying which components and sub-assemblies are required to manufacture a parent product. | Each BOM entry indicates a parent product, a component product (which can be either a component or sub-assembly), the required quantity, unit of measure, and hierarchical level in the product structure. | BOM connects Products to other Products (parent-child).
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: The Bill of Materials (BOM) defines the composition of products by specifying which components and sub-assemblies are required to manufacture a parent product. | Each BOM entry indicates a parent product, a component product (which can be either a component or sub-assembly), the required quantity, unit of measure, and hierarchical level in the product structure. | BOM connects Products to other Products (parent-child).

entities_post_resolution.definition: - QC results determine Batch status
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: - QC results determine Batch status

entities_post_resolution.definition: | component_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → component(component_id) | Component reference (exclusive with product_id) |
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | component_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → component(component_id) | Component reference (exclusive with product_id) |

entities_post_resolution.definition: bom_level | INT | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0, CHECK >= 0 | Depth in product hierarchy

entities_post_resolution.name: Defined Capacity Limits
entities_post_resolution.definition: Warehouses have defined capacity limits, geographic location information, and are managed by designated managers.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Warehouses have defined capacity limits, geographic location information, and are managed by designated managers.

entities_post_resolution.name: Either Components Or Products
entities_post_resolution.definition: Inventory records support either components or products (but not both in the same record) and include timestamps for the last restock date.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Inventory records support either components or products (but not both in the same record) and include timestamps for the last restock date.

entities_post_resolution.name: Multiple Suppliers
entities_post_resolution.definition: Components are supplied by multiple Suppliers (many-to-many).
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Components are supplied by multiple Suppliers (many-to-many).

entities_post_resolution.name: Multiple Child Products
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Product can have multiple child Products (via BOM hierarchy).
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: A Product can have multiple child Products (via BOM hierarchy).

entities_post_resolution.name: Negotiated Pricing And
entities_post_resolution.definition: Suppliers have negotiated pricing and lead times per component.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Suppliers have negotiated pricing and lead times per component.

entities_post_resolution.name: Nodes In The
entities_post_resolution.definition: Warehouses serve as nodes in the supply chain network where inventory is held, shipments are received, and products are staged for production or distribution.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Warehouses serve as nodes in the supply chain network where inventory is held, shipments are received, and products are staged for production or distribution.

tables_enriched.name: Product
tables_enriched.description: This table stores information about products, including their identifiers, names, types, hierarchical relationships, costs, lead times, and active status.

tables_enriched.name: Component
tables_enriched.description: This table stores information about various components used in manufacturing or assembly processes, including their identifiers, names, categories, measurement units, costs, and related specifications... [TRUNCATED]

tables_enriched.name: Bill of Materials
tables_enriched.description: This table stores the components and structure of a product's bill of materials, detailing which components are used in each product and their quantities.

tables_enriched.name: Supplier
tables_enriched.description: This table stores information about suppliers providing goods or services to the company, including contact details and preference status.

tables_enriched.name: Warehouse
tables_enriched.description: This table stores information about warehouses, including their locations, capacities, and managers responsible for each warehouse.

tables_enriched.name: Inventory
tables_enriched.description: This table tracks inventory levels and restocking information for products stored across various warehouses.

tables_enriched.name: Work Order
tables_enriched.description: This table stores information about manufacturing or maintenance work orders, including their status, priorities, associated products, quantities, and scheduling details.

tables_enriched.name: Shipment
tables_enriched.description: This table records details about shipments, including their types, associated warehouses, suppliers, customers, and key dates related to shipment progress and status.

tables_enriched.name: Quality Control
tables_enriched.description: This table stores records of quality control inspections, including details about batch testing, inspection results, and defect counts to ensure product quality standards.

tables_enriched.name: Specification
tables_enriched.description: This table stores specifications details, including versioning, effective dates, and critical parameters for product or process standards.

tables_enriched.name: Batch
tables_enriched.description: This table records information about production batches of products, including quantities, production dates, warehouse locations, and quality control status.

tables_enriched.name: Route
tables_enriched.description: This table stores information about manufacturing or process routes, including the sequence of operations, associated work centers, and timing details for each route.

tables_enriched.name: Component Supplier
tables_enriched.description: This table stores information about the suppliers for each component, including preference status, lead time, and pricing details.
